2605.08242.title: An Explainable Unsupervised-to-Supervised Machine Learning Framework for Dietary Pattern Discovery Using UK National Dietary Survey Data
2605.08242.paper_summary: Clinical dietary assessment can generate detailed but high-dimensional nutrient and food-group information that is difficult to translate quickly into counselling priorities. This paper proposes an explainable unsupervised-to-supervised machine learning framework for discovering, reproducing and interpreting dietary patterns using public UK National Diet and Nutrition Survey data. Adult participants aged 19 years and above from NDNS Years 12-15 were represented using 25 energy-adjusted nutrient and food-group features. K-means, Gaussian Mixture Models and Agglomerative Clustering were compared across k = 2-8, with stability and dietetic interpretability used alongside internal validation metrics. The selected K-means k = 4 solution identified four interpretable dietary patterns: high fat/meat and sodium, higher fibre fruit-vegetable micronutrient, high free-sugar snacks and sugary drinks, and dairy/cereal calcium-rich saturated-fat. A supervised surrogate classifier reproduced held-out cluster membership with high test performance (macro-F1 = 0.963), but was interpreted only as an explanatory surrogate rather than as an independent clinical prediction model. SHAP analysis linked predictions to dietetically meaningful drivers, suggesting potential value for dietitian-in-the-loop assessment, counselling prioritisation and follow-up monitoring.
2605.08242.paper_summary_zh: 臨床飲食評估可以生成詳細但高維度的營養和食品組別信息，這些信息難以迅速轉化為輔導優先事項。本文提出了一種可解釋的無監督到監督的機器學習框架，用於利用公共英國國家飲食和營養調查數據發現、重現和解釋飲食模式。來自NDNS第12-15年的19歲及以上成人參與者使用25個經能量調整的營養和食品組別特徵進行表示。在k = 2-8的範圍內比較了K-means、高斯混合模型和聚合聚類，並使用穩定性和飲食解釋性與內部驗證指標一起進行評估。所選擇的K-means k = 4解決方案識別了四種可解釋的飲食模式：高脂肪/肉類和鈉、高纖維水果-蔬菜微量營養素、高自由糖零食和含糖飲料，以及乳製品/穀物鈣豐富的飽和脂肪。一個監督的替代分類器以高測試性能（宏F1 = 0.963）重現了保留的聚類成員資格，但僅被解釋為解釋性替代，而不是作為獨立的臨床預測模型。SHAP分析將預測與飲食上有意義的驅動因素聯繫起來，表明對於營養師介入的評估、輔導優先級和後續監測具有潛在價值。
2605.08242.author: Wing Yi Yu et.al.
2605.08242.authors: Wing Yi Yu, Chun Yin Chiu
